a:T74b0,<div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>The graph shows blood pressure changes on the left side of the heart during one heartbeat. Identify the <strong>two</strong> parts of the circulatory system that produce traces I and II on the graph.</p>
<p style="text-align: center;"><img src="data:image/png;base64,"/></p>
<p style="text-align: center;">[Source: © International Baccalaureate Organization 2019]</p>
<div class="marks">[2]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Outline the actions taken by the body to avoid infection when the skin is cut.</p>
<div class="marks">[3]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Hormones are distributed throughout the body by the blood. Outline the roles of <strong>two</strong> reproductive hormones during the menstrual cycle in women.</p>
<div class="marks">[2]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">c.</div>
</div>b:T84d,<div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>I. aorta ✔</p>
<p>II: «left» atrium ✔</p>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>a. platelets/cut tissues release clotting factors ✔ <em>Mp a requires student to identify source of clotting factors.</em></p>
<p>b. «clotting factors» activate thrombin «from prothrombin» ✔</p>
<p>c. thrombin converts fibrinogen to fibrin ✔</p>
<p>d. «fibrin» forms a clot/scab/mesh that seals the cut ✔</p>
<p>e. phagocytic white blood cells ingest pathogens ✔</p>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>a. FSH/follicle stimulating hormone stimulates the development of follicles/follicle cell division in the ovary «to produce eggs» ✔ <span style="display: inline !important;float: none;background-color: #ffffff;color: #000000;font-family: Verdana,Arial,Helvetica,sans-serif;font-size: 14px;font-style: italic;font-variant: normal;font-weight: 400;letter-spacing: normal;text-align: left;text-decoration: none;text-indent: 0px;white-space: normal;">Two different hormones must be identified. </span></p>
<p>b. LH/luteinizing hormone triggers ovulation/development of the corpus luteum ✔</p>
<p>c. estrogen stimulates development of the uterine lining/endometrium ✔  <span style="display: inline !important;float: none;background-color: #ffffff;color: #000000;font-family: Verdana,Arial,Helvetica,sans-serif;font-size: 14px;font-style: italic;font-variant: normal;font-weight: 400;letter-spacing: normal;text-align: left;text-decoration: none;text-indent: 0px;white-space: normal;">Description of role required as well as name of hormone.</span></p>
<p>d. progesterone maintains the uterine lining/endometrium<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>inhibits other hormones by negative feedback <em>eg</em>, FSH ✔</p>
<p>e. HCG stimulates ovary to produce progesterone «in early pregnancy» ✔</p>
<p>f. other verifiable hormone and roles relevant to the menstrual cycle ✔</p>
<div class="question_part_label">c.</div>
</div>2:["$","article",null,{"className":"q-page","children":[["$","p",null,{"style":{"margin":0},"children":["$","$L6",null,{"href":"/subject/biology/topic/6/","children":["← back to ","Biology"," topic ","6"]}]}],["$","h1",null,{"children":"19M.2.HL.TZ2.3"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-meta","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"tag","children":"pestle"}],["$","span",null,{"children":"Biology"}],null,["$","span",null,{"children":"HL"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["Paper ","2"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"19M"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["· ","6-2-the-blood-system, 6-3-defence-against-infectious-disease, 6-6-hormones-homeostasis-and-reproduction"]}],["$","a",null,{"href":"https://pestle.pages.dev/app/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener","children":"source ↗"}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-content","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$a"}}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Markscheme / solution"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$b"}}]]}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Examiners’ report"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<p>This question was poorly answered with most students earning either one mark or no marks. It was more common for candidates to correctly identify the atrium rather than the aorta. ß</p>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">a.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<p>This question about clotting was well answered with most being able to correctly identify the roles of clotting factors, fibrinogen and thrombin, though in a number of cases, specific details were lacking.</p>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">b.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<p>Students were able to correctly identify hormones that played a role in the menstrual cycle and were often successful in outlining their function. A common problem was distinguishing the role of progesterone from that of estrogen.</p>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">c.</div>\n</div>"}}]]}]]}]
